summary:
Claim: Cholesterol is the most abundant lipid component in HDL.  
Evidence: - HDL specializes in reverse cholesterol transport, requiring high cholesterol content to remove excess cholesterol from arterial walls. - Unlike VLDL or chylomicrons, which are rich in triglycerides, HDL has a high cholesterol-to-triglyceride ratio.  
Answer: A
